name: causl-monorepo
description: Transactional state for tangled dependency graphs.

scripts.validate: pnpm typecheck && pnpm build && pnpm test:run && pnpm docs:test
scripts.corpus:report: pnpm --filter @causl/core exec vitest run test/properties/failing-against-stub.property.test.ts --reporter=json 2>/dev/null | node scripts/corpus-report.mjs

//size-limit: Per SPEC.md §14.2 the bundle budget is the 15-30 KB band, with 18 KB as the working target on @causl/core (full import). The ceilings below are CI-gate caps — a PR that crosses one fails `size — bundle-size gate` and must include the §14.2.1 written team consensus or the size-limit bump is rejected. The 4.5 KB / 6 KB / 3 KB ceilings the previous spec defended were retired in PR #458 once the team accepted that the small-bundle promise was costing more in adopter glue (per closed PRs #266, #395, #420, #383, #455, #390, #454) than it was earning in elegance.
//size-limit-wasm: The per-bridge wasm-pkg artefact cells (serde-json/gc-builtins/gc-classic) were activated by Sub-E (#1063) closeout per the corrected #689 budgets. Each cell asserts the raw .wasm artefact ceiling; the Brotli q11 ceiling is enforced post-compress at `pnpm wasm:build` upload time and is documented in `packages/core/wasm-pkg/README.md`. Per #1103 the per-bridge artefact tree was split into `<bridge>-bundler/` (consumed by the @causl/core/wasm loader + bundler-interop fixtures) and `<bridge>-nodejs/` (consumed by Node test runners such as the bridge-roundtrip property suite). wasm-pack emits byte-identical `.wasm` from both targets, so the size-limit cells gate the `-bundler` variant only — a per-target row pair would duplicate the cap with no extra signal. The 8-byte WASM-preamble stubs committed under both `<bridge>-bundler/` and `<bridge>-nodejs/` let the cells gate today; `pnpm wasm:build` replaces them with real artefacts and the caps bite from that point on. If #692's `js-sys`-drop path lands, `gc-builtins` ratchets to ≤70 KB raw / ≤28 KB Brotli — file a follow-up issue for the ratchet. NOTE (#1150): the `@causl/core wasm bridge — serde-json (raw)` cell sits at 230 KB to absorb the current 213 KB serde artefact, which exceeds the SPEC §17.6 commitment-14 ceiling of 200 KB raw by 13 KB (Brotli 66 KB remains under the 80 KB target). The gap is documented as Option C divergence in §17.6's current-state paragraph; the cell tightens back to ≤200 KB when the Rust engine port (epic #1133) lands and wasm-opt is invoked directly per PR #1112's design discussion — see #1150 for the resolution path.
